learningObjectives.title: Understand carrier selection
learningObjectives.description: Explain carrier evaluation using rates, service, availability and agreements.

learningObjectives.title: Understand assignment methods
learningObjectives.description: Differentiate manual, automatic, tendering and contract-based assignment.

learningObjectives.title: Differentiate carrier and resource assignment
learningObjectives.description: Clarify selecting transport company versus assigning physical assets.

sections.content: Carrier Selection is the process of choosing the most suitable transportation service provider to execute a shipment. SAP TM evaluates multiple carriers using predefined business rules such as freight rates, transit time, service level, carrier availability and contractual agreements before assigning a carrier to a Freight Order.

sections.title: Key Concept
sections.content: A Carrier is a Business Partner that provides transportation services. Carrier Selection occurs after transportation planning and before transportation execution.

sections.title: Carrier Selection Factors
rows: Road, Rail, Air or Ocean support

sections.title: Assignment Types
rows: SAP TM selects carrier based on rules

sections.content: A company needs to transport electronics from Bengaluru to Mumbai. SAP TM evaluates three approved carriers based on freight rates, delivery time and historical performance. The system automatically assigns the carrier that offers the lowest cost while meeting the required delivery date.

sections.title: Best Practice
sections.content: Maintain carrier performance metrics and freight agreements regularly so SAP TM can make accurate carrier selection decisions.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: An automotive manufacturer implemented automatic carrier selection using freight agreements and carrier KPIs. Manual carrier assignment reduced by 90%, while transportation costs decreased by 12%.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: Remember that a Carrier is maintained as a Business Partner in SAP TM. Carrier Selection uses business rules, freight agreements and performance indicators, not random selection.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: Do not confuse Carrier Selection with Resource Assignment. Carrier Selection chooses the transportation company, while Resource Assignment allocates the physical vehicle or equipment.

items: Carrier Selection determines the transportation provider.
Carriers are Business Partners in SAP TM.
Selection can be manual, automatic or tender-based.
Carrier selection considers cost, transit time and service level.
Resource Assignment occurs after Carrier Selection.
